demographics.living_situation: lives at home; an adult child is present and helping

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I've had gallbladder pain before, but it usually goes away after a few hours. This time it's been constant all night and the pain shoots right through to my shoulder blade.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The pain has been constant for hours and is much worse than my usual attacks, I keep being sick and can't keep any fluids down, and I feel hot and shivery. My adult child called for help because I can't get comfortable.

symptom_narrative.onset: came on after a fatty meal about 8 hours ago
symptom_narrative.duration: constant for more than 8 hours — my usual attacks settle in a couple of hours
symptom_narrative.character: severe, constant, gripping pain high up on the right side of my belly, boring straight through to my right shoulder blade
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst it's ever been — I can't lie still, I keep writhing around trying to get comfortable
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: top right of my tummy, up under the ribs, going through to my right shoulder blade
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant now, not coming and going in waves like it used to
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing helps — the paracetamol and ibuprofen I took a few hours ago barely touched it
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: moving around and being sick make it worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: high fever and chills, feeling really hot, a lot of nausea and vomiting, no appetite at all, and feeling completely wiped out
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've had gallbladder attacks before but they always settled within a few hours on their own
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I'm stuck in bed, can't get comfortable, and can't keep any fluids down

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: gallstones — I was told they were there but they never really caused much trouble
past_medical_history.how_managed: no treatment, just kept an eye on

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives at home; an adult child is present and made the call for help

objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single reading on a home thermometer

objective_data_offered.value: she looks flushed and sweaty, is shivering, and is obviously in a lot of pain — she can't keep still
she has vomited several times and can't keep even water down
several empty antacid packets on the bedside table
